title: cross-vendor ONNX embedder backend (OrtBgeEmbedder)
summary: OrtBgeEmbedder (mod ort_bge) implemented behind the trait + NON-default onnx-embedder feature; R-ONNX-2 runtime device->ORT-provider mapping done and unit-tested. R-ONNX-1 real-vector test is #[ignore] because no offline bge-small .onnx model + ONNX Runtime native lib exist in the build/eval envelope (see blocked_on).
ort_version_note: The ort 2.0 line is the maintained series (1.x is EOL) but ships only as release candidates. rc.10 is the latest rc that builds cleanly in the offline envelope: rc.12 fails to compile (ort/ort-sys VitisAI EP field mismatch, E0609). Pinned exact (=) to avoid silently resolving to a broken rc. ADR-0.8.16-onnx-embedder-backend section 5.2 explicitly flags ort-version acceptance as an HITL open item -- surfacing here for confirmation.
ort_features_note: default-features = false + load-dynamic: the ONNX Runtime native lib is dlopen'd at RUNTIME (ORT_DYLIB_PATH), so cargo build/check never downloads or links a native binary at build time (no download-binaries). load-dynamic transitively pulls std -> ndarray + libloading; output extraction uses try_extract_tensor (no ndarray API surface used directly).
files_touched: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-embedder/src/lib.rs (mod gating + re-export; device.rs cfg extended to onnx-embedder)
Cargo.lock (lockfile update for ort/ort-sys/tokenizers under the new optional dep)
engine_diff: NONE — git diff 146eecca -- src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine is empty (0 lines). Injection is caller-side via EmbedderChoice::Caller; the engine never names OrtBgeEmbedder.

footprint_invariant.check: cargo tree -p fathomdb-embedder (default features = []) contains NO ort / ort-sys / tokenizers / onnx entry (grep exit 1 = absent). ort + tokenizers appear only under --features onnx-embedder. Default cargo check -p fathomdb-embedder exits 0 with zero new deps.
footprint_invariant.default_features: default = [] stays ML-free; onnx-embedder = [dep:ort, dep:tokenizers] is NON-default.

tests.result: 7 passed, 1 ignored (cargo test -p fathomdb-embedder --features onnx-embedder ort_bge)

r_onnx_1_note: Code path fully implemented (from_files/from_env constructor -> tokenize (bge tokenizer.json, trunc 512) -> ORT Session forward -> CLS/Mean pool -> L2-normalize -> 384-dim Vector via the Embedder trait). Real-vector assertion is #[ignore] pending an offline model+runtime (blocked_on). Distinct identity 'fathomdb-bge-small-en-v1.5-onnx' structurally enforces R-ONNX-3 same-backend read discipline.
r_onnx_2_note: DONE + tested. parse_device_request (shared grammar, unchanged) -> map_device_request -> OrtProvider {Cpu|Cuda|Rocm|DirectMl|OpenVino}, resolved at RUNTIME from FATHOMDB_EMBED_DEVICE (not compile-time). Cross-vendor providers (rocm|rocm:N|directml|dml|openvino|ovep) are requested via the grammar's Unknown arm so the shared parser is untouched; metal + unrecognized tokens are LOUD CPU fallbacks (warning surfaced to stderr at construction, never in embed()).
r_onnx_3_note: Interim guard is documentation-level per ADR (not enforced until 0.8.18 #5). Enforced structurally here via a distinct embedder identity name so the engine identity check prevents silently reading candle-written vectors with the ONNX backend. Actual candle<->ONNX delta MEASUREMENT is Slice 15, not this slice.
pooling_note: OrtPooling::{Mean,Cls} mirrors candle_bge::Pooling; default Cls (the CLS-corrected mode the Slice-15 equivalence is measured against). with_pooling() allows Mean for the harness. DECISION surfaced for review.

gates.eu7: NOT RUN (correct — default candle CPU path unchanged, no-op basis per ADR section 4)

fix_1.finding: [P2] silent CPU fallback breaks the R-ONNX-2 loud-fallback contract: from_files_with_provider built the session with .with_execution_providers([provider_dispatch(provider)]) and relied on ort's NON-FATAL dispatch fallback, but ort is built default-features=false + load-dynamic, so ort's own warn/error macros are compiled out. Requesting an unavailable GPU provider (e.g. rocm on a CPU-only dylib, directml on Linux) therefore fell back to CPU SILENTLY, making a cross-vendor run look successful while secretly on CPU.
fix_1.mechanism: Added a SESSION-BUILD-stage loud fallback that is OUR OWN, not reliant on ort's compiled-out macros. Pure decision fn resolve_effective_provider_with(requested, is_available) -> (OrtProvider, Option<String>): a non-CPU provider the probe reports unavailable is downgraded to Cpu with a warning that NAMES the requested provider and states the CPU fallback; CPU requests return (Cpu, None) and never probe. Thin live wrapper resolve_effective_provider() probes via ort's ExecutionProvider::is_available() (per-EP builder), mapping a probe error (e.g. absent dylib under load-dynamic) to unavailable. from_files_with_provider now calls resolve_effective_provider(provider) BEFORE committing the session, emits the warning via emit_onnx_warning (shared eprintln helper, clippy::print_stderr allow scoped to construction, never embed()), and dispatches the EFFECTIVE provider so CPU functionality is preserved. Mirrors the candle path's loud CPU fallback. Model/asset path and ort footprint (default-features=false + load-dynamic, no tracing) unchanged; default build stays ML-free.
tests_added: ort_bge::tests::requested_gpu_unavailable_falls_back_to_cpu_loudly (Cuda/Rocm/DirectMl/OpenVino with injected |_| false probe -> (Cpu, Some(warning)) naming the requested provider + stating CPU) — PASS
ort_bge::tests::requested_available_provider_is_honored_without_warning (injected |_| true -> requested honored, no warning) — PASS
ort_bge::tests::cpu_request_never_probes_and_never_warns (probe closure panics if called; CPU -> (Cpu, None)) — PASS
fix_1.tests_result: cargo test -p fathomdb-embedder --features onnx-embedder ort_bge: 10 passed, 1 ignored (the 7 original device-mapping tests + 3 new fallback tests; R-ONNX-1 real-vector test stays #[ignore], asset blocker unchanged).
fix_1.model_independent: Pure fn probe is injected, so the 3 new tests need no model file and no ORT native lib — they run despite the R-ONNX-1 asset blocker.
files_touched: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-embedder/src/ort_bge.rs (the code fix; output.json is the closure witness in the same commit)
fix_1.engine_diff: STILL NONE — git diff 55125959 -- src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine is empty.

gates.default_tree_no_ort_tokenizers: confirmed (cargo tree -p fathomdb-embedder default: no ort/tokenizers)

fix_2.finding: [P2] deeper case of the loud-fallback contract: fix-1's is_available() availability probe covers the compiled-in check, but even when ORT reports a non-CPU EP as compiled-in, Session::builder().with_execution_providers([provider_dispatch(effective)]).commit_from_file(...) can STILL fail at RUNTIME (missing CUDA/cuDNN/ROCm runtime libs, bad device id, incompatible driver). ort surfaces that as a hard Err from the constructor, so a user with a CUDA-enabled ORT build but absent runtime deps could not open the embedder AT ALL — violating the documented loud CPU fallback for unavailable providers.
fix_2.mechanism: Factored the session-build control flow into a pure/generic helper build_session_with_fallback<S,E,F>(effective: OrtProvider, build: F) -> Result<(S, Option<String>), E> where F: Fn(OrtProvider) -> Result<S,E>, E: Display. It attempts build(effective); on Ok returns (session, None); on Err of a NON-CPU effective provider it composes a LOUD warning naming the requested provider + the error + the CPU fallback, RETRIES build(OrtProvider::Cpu), and returns the CPU session with the warning (only a CPU-retry failure is fatal); on Err of a CPU-effective provider it returns Err with NO retry. from_files_with_provider is now a thin caller passing the real ORT build closure (Session::builder()? .with_execution_providers([provider_dispatch(p)])? .commit_from_file(model_path)), mapping E via err("session build", e), and emits build_warn via the existing emit_onnx_warning helper. fix-1's availability probe + warnings are UNCHANGED; model/asset path unchanged; ort footprint (default-features=false + load-dynamic) unchanged; default build stays ML-free.
tests_added: ort_bge::tests::session_build_gpu_fails_retries_cpu_with_warning (effective GPU {Cuda/Rocm/OpenVino}, injected closure fails for GPU + succeeds for CPU -> returns CPU session + warning naming the provider, attempts GPU-then-CPU exactly) — PASS
ort_bge::tests::session_build_gpu_and_cpu_both_fail_errors (injected closure fails for every provider, effective Cuda -> Err) — PASS
ort_bge::tests::session_build_cpu_failure_is_error_without_retry (effective CPU, closure fails -> Err, build closure called exactly once) — PASS
ort_bge::tests::session_build_success_no_warning_single_attempt (effective Cuda, closure succeeds -> (session, None), single attempt) — PASS
fix_2.tests_result: cargo test -p fathomdb-embedder --features onnx-embedder ort_bge: 14 passed, 1 ignored (10 prior + 4 new session-build-retry tests; R-ONNX-1 real-vector test stays #[ignore], asset blocker unchanged).
fix_2.model_independent: The retry control flow is exercised via an injected build closure over a fake session/error type (Result<&str, String>), so the 4 new tests need no model file and no ORT native lib — they run despite the R-ONNX-1 asset blocker.
files_touched: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-embedder/src/ort_bge.rs (the code fix; output.json is the closure witness in the same commit)
fix_2.engine_diff: STILL NONE — git diff 146eecca -- src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine is empty.

gates.default_tree_no_ort_tokenizers: confirmed (cargo tree -p fathomdb-embedder default: no ort/tokenizers)

fix_3.finding: [P2] ROOT cause that made fix-1/fix-2's loud-fallback machinery never fire at runtime: provider_dispatch(p) returned an ExecutionProviderDispatch that defaults to fail_silently (error_on_failure = false). So .with_execution_providers([...]) returned Ok even when a compiled-in non-CPU provider (CUDA/ROCm/DirectML/OpenVINO) could NOT REGISTER at runtime (host missing CUDA/cuDNN/ROCm libs, bad device id) — ORT logs internally and silently falls back to CPU. build_session_with_fallback therefore never saw an Err, build_warn stayed None, and the run silently used CPU, defeating the entire loud-fallback contract.
fix_3.mechanism: In provider_dispatch, every NON-CPU provider dispatch is now built with .error_on_failure() (verified present on ExecutionProviderDispatch in the pinned ort =2.0.0-rc.10: src/execution_providers/mod.rs:187 — sets error_on_failure=true so a failed EP registration surfaces as Err from with_execution_providers rather than silently falling back). A non-CPU registration failure now flows into build_session_with_fallback's existing CPU-retry + LOUD warning path. The CPU dispatch KEEPS the default (fail_silently) behavior: CPU is the always-available floor and the retry target, so it must be allowed to succeed. No change to the model/asset path, the ort footprint (default-features=false + load-dynamic), or the default ML-free build.
fix_3.ort_api_confirmed: ExecutionProviderDispatch::error_on_failure(self) -> Self exists in ort =2.0.0-rc.10 (execution_providers/mod.rs:187, doc: 'return an error upon EP registration if registration of this EP fails'; the default new() sets error_on_failure=false). Method name matches the finding; no equivalent substitution needed.
tests_added: ort_bge::tests::non_cpu_dispatch_errors_on_failure_cpu_does_not (asserts provider_dispatch({Cuda,Rocm,DirectMl,OpenVino}) renders 'error_on_failure: true' via the dispatch Debug output and CPU renders 'error_on_failure: false'; .build() only constructs the dispatch config struct, so no ORT native lib is loaded) — PASS
fix_3.tests_result: cargo test -p fathomdb-embedder --features onnx-embedder ort_bge: 15 passed, 1 ignored (14 prior + 1 new dispatch-flag test; R-ONNX-1 real-vector test stays #[ignore], asset blocker unchanged).
fix_3.live_path_note: The live end-to-end registration-failure -> Err -> CPU-retry -> loud warning path is exercised only when a real ORT native lib + model are present; that is verified at the Slice-15 / MAIN-tree run with a real ORT, part of the R-ONNX-1 blocked-asset work below. The unit test proves the dispatch is now wired with error_on_failure (the precondition for the live path to fire).
files_touched: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-embedder/src/ort_bge.rs (the code fix; output.json is the closure witness in the same commit)
fix_3.engine_diff: STILL NONE — git diff 146eecca -- src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine is empty.

gates.default_tree_no_ort_tokenizers: confirmed (cargo tree -p fathomdb-embedder default: no ort/tokenizers)

blocked_on.item: R-ONNX-1 real-vector test (the fixture-text -> 384-dim vector assertion) requires a decision the implementer cannot make within scope.
missing_assets: A BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5 .onnx model file. The offline envelope has ONLY candle safetensors (~/.cache/huggingface/hub/models--BAAI--bge-small-en-v1.5 and ~/.cache/fathomdb/embedders/*). No bge-small .onnx exists anywhere on the host. Obtaining one needs either network egress (HF onnx/model.onnx) or an offline ONNX export of the pinned safetensors.

An ONNX Runtime native shared library reachable via ORT_DYLIB_PATH. The only onnxruntime .so files on the host are bundled inside Python venvs (memex .venv onnxruntime 1.23.2; /tmp/gtest-venv onnxruntime 1.27.0). Reusing a Python-wheel .so as the load-dynamic target is fragile/undecided; vendoring a native ORT binary is a size/licensing decision.
blocked_on.decision_needed: HITL to (a) confirm the ort version pin (=2.0.0-rc.10, or accept the rc line at all), and (b) authorize a source for the offline bge-small .onnx model + ONNX Runtime native lib (network egress at eval time, an offline export step, or a vendored/pointed runtime). Once provisioned, run: FATHOMDB_ONNX_MODEL_PATH=... FATHOMDB_ONNX_TOKENIZER_PATH=... ORT_DYLIB_PATH=... cargo test -p fathomdb-embedder --features onnx-embedder -- --ignored
blocked_on.not_blocking: This does NOT block the code, feature-gating, footprint invariant, or R-ONNX-2 device mapping — all implemented + tested + committed. The candle<->ONNX delta measurement (R-ONNX-3) is Slice 15 regardless.
